steps.title: Complete the noun phrase
steps.content: The sentence is describing what Wole Soyinka and Olga Tokarczuk were granted. The blank must complete the phrase the most prestigious award for a person of letters and then introduce the specific award named at the end: the Nobel Prize in Literature. The punctuation has to keep award for a person of letters together before introducing that name.

steps.title: Reject misplaced separators
steps.content: Choice A, award for a person of letters;, uses a semicolon before the Nobel Prize in Literature, but that phrase is not an independent clause. Choice B, award: for a person of letters, puts the colon too early and splits the noun phrase. Choice D, award; for a person of letters., separates the phrase with a semicolon and then cuts off the named prize with a period.

steps.title: Use the colon after the phrase
steps.content: Choice C, award for a person of letters:, completes the full noun phrase before the colon. The colon then introduces the Nobel Prize in Literature as the specific example of that prestigious award. This keeps the grammar intact and makes the final noun phrase explain what the award is. The answer is C.
